The current owner, a eccentric old man named Mr. Jenkins, had finally agreed to list the property after years of persuasion. I had heard whispers of its fascinating history - from its origins as a 19th-century boarding house to its rumored connections to the city's underground jazz scene. As I approached the entrance, I noticed the intricate carvings on the doorframe, the stained glass windows that filtered the afternoon light, and the faint scent of old books and decay. This was going to be a challenging sale, but I was ready to uncover the secrets hidden within these walls and find the perfect buyer to appreciate its unique charm.
